topic: Arrays - IntoIterator for [T; N]: Owned Array Iteration

turns.content: I copied this code from a tutorial but I'm getting an error where the array is being treated as borrowed instead of owned. The message says 'the trait bound for &mut T failed' and it seems like my loop isn't consuming the elements properly even though I thought arrays should work differently than Vecs.

The error happens because Rust distinguishes between the array type [T; N] which owns its data, and slices &mut [T] which only borrow. Since Rust 1.53 arrays implement IntoIterator directly to yield owned T values, whereas calling into_iter on a slice yields borrowed references. You need to ensure you are iterating over the concrete array type rather than slicing it first. When you iterate with for x in arr you move elements out of storage while a manual loop using &mut creates a borrow that prevents further mutation or collection back into an array. Can you show me exactly which line is causing the trait bound failure so I can pinpoint whether you are accidentally borrowing before iterating?
